actions.detail: role 'Dispute Intake Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Fraud Investigator' bound to permission group
role 'Chargeback Specialist' bound to permission group
role 'Dispute Operations Manager' bound to permission group
role 'Dispute Compliance Auditor' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: Dispute-domain policies (no outcome promised to a cardholder before the human determination; denial letters must cite actual investigation findings; no provisional-credit reversal without the Reg E notice; UDAAP pattern-of-denial watch) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route final determinations exclusively to human analysts with the Dispute Operations Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Dispute Intake Copilot, Chargeback Representment Builder, First-Party Fraud Scorer, Provisional Credit Clock Sentinel) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (core processors: FIS/Fiserv/TSYS/Marqeta/ Galileo; network dispute rails: Visa Resolve Online (VROL), Mastercom; fraud platforms; ATM operator journals; carrier tracking APIs for delivery-confirmation evidence) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (post/reverse provisional credit on the core, submit chargeback/representment via VROL/Mastercom, order card reissue, send Reg E/Z status and resolution letters) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous credit movement or network filing, ever.

Golden eval sets (dispute triage, deadline-risk ranking, chargeback reason-code selection, first-party fraud escalation) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Cardholder, Account, Card, Transaction, Dispute, Chargeback, Representment, ProvisionalCredit, Merchant, ReasonCode, DeadlineClock, ResolutionLetter) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the disputes_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (dispute_intake, fraud_investigation, chargeback_representment, atm_error_review, first_party_fraud_review) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (dispute_outcome_confidence, first_party_fraud_score) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest transaction anomaly + xgboost dispute-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Dispute", "Chargeback", "Provisional credit due", "Deadline at risk", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
